scenario: Nimbus Bank processes 60000 records per day in Camunda 8.8. A FEEL input mapping on a service task must extract a 4 character window from an account reference starting at the third character of the reference, and must safely return whatever characters remain when only 2 characters remain after the start position.
question: Which substring call returns a 4 character window starting at the third position and returns the remaining characters when fewer than 4 remain?

options.text: substring(value, 3, 4) returns a window of length 4 starting from position 3 using zero based indexing and pads the result with spaces when short
substring(value, 4, 3) returns a window of length 3 starting from position 4 using one based indexing and returns null when fewer characters remain
substring(value, 3, 4) returns a window of length 4 starting from position 3 using one based indexing and returns the remainder when fewer remain
substring(value, -3, 4) returns a window of length 4 starting from the third position from the end and returns null when fewer characters remain

a.text: Incorrect. substring uses one based indexing where the first index is 1, not 0; treating the third character as index 2 with zero based indexing does not match the documented start position rule, and the function does not pad short results with spaces.

b.text: Incorrect. The order is substring(value, start position, length); a length of 3 from position 4 returns three characters from the fourth character, which does not satisfy the four-character requirement at position 3.

c.text: Correct. substring uses one based indexing where the first character is at position 1, so substring(value, 3, 4) returns up to 4 characters starting at the third character, and when fewer than 4 remain it returns all characters from the start position to the end.

d.text: Incorrect. A negative start position counts from the end of the value, not from the front; substring(value, -3, 4) starts three characters before the end rather than at the third character.

explanation: substring(string, start position, length) uses 1 as the first index and -1 as the last index. If length is greater than the remaining characters from start position, the function returns all characters from start position through the end.
